assertions.expectation: Recommends combining release channels with maintenance exclusions as the approach for maximum control
assertions.reason: Response explicitly recommends 'Extended Channel + No Minor or Node Exclusion' configuration and provides specific gcloud commands combining both concepts

assertions.expectation: Recommends 'no minor or node upgrades' exclusion to prevent disruptive changes while allowing control plane security patches. May also mention persistent exclusions with --add-maintenance-exclusion-until-end-of-support (no longer a 6-month max, auto-tracks EoS)
assertions.reason: Response specifically recommends 'no minor or node upgrades' exclusion, includes --add-maintenance-exclusion-until-end-of-support flag in the command, and explains how this allows CP patches while blocking disruptive changes

assertions.expectation: Recommends maintenance windows to control WHEN patches are applied (e.g., weekend windows only)
assertions.reason: Response includes maintenance window configuration with Saturday 2-6 AM window (--maintenance-window-start and --maintenance-window-recurrence), and shows multi-environment strategy with different maintenance windows

assertions.expectation: Mentions the cluster disruption budget (disruption interval) to control upgrade frequency — patch interval default 7d (max 90d), minor interval default 30d (max 90d)
assertions.reason: Response includes --maintenance-patch-version-disruption-interval=7776000s (90 days) in the configuration and explicitly mentions '90-day patch disruption interval' to limit patches to once every 90 days maximum

assertions.expectation: Suggests Stable or Extended channel for production clusters in regulated environments. Extended channel provides maximum flexibility around EoS enforcement (additional cost only during extended period)
assertions.reason: Response explicitly recommends Extended channel, explains extended support (24 months vs 14), mentions cost optimization where Extended channel fee only applies during months 15-24, and compares with Stable channel

assertions.expectation: Notes that disabling control plane patches entirely is not recommended due to security risks — monthly patches at minimum
assertions.reason: Response emphasizes that control plane security patches are auto-applied in the recommended configuration and explains why this is important for security compliance, avoiding complete patch disabling

assertions.expectation: May mention rollout sequencing as an advanced option for controlling upgrade order across multiple clusters, and/or the cluster disruption budget for controlling CP patch frequency
assertions.reason: Response includes a 'Multi-Environment Strategy' section showing dev → staging → prod progression with different maintenance windows for natural sequencing, and mentions cluster disruption budget (disruption interval) for controlling patch frequency

assertions.expectation: Provides gcloud commands for configuring the recommended exclusion type and maintenance window
assertions.reason: Response provides multiple detailed gcloud commands including cluster update commands with exclusion scopes, maintenance windows, and complete configuration examples
